comprehension_gate.instruction:
READ THIS ENTIRE PROMPT before posting your comprehension gate. Then READ all files listed in required_attachments. Then POST a comprehension gate comment on Vikunja Task 82, prefixed:
[agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension]
Your comprehension comment MUST contain ALL of the following sections (verbatim section headers required, in this exact order):
A. MILESTONE OBJECTIVE (in your own words, 3-5 sentences) B. WORK ITEMS (one sentence each for ALL 14 WIs — do not summarize or group) C. FILES TO CREATE (list every new test file) D. FILES TO MODIFY (list every existing file you will add tests to) E. FILES TO READ (all production source files you will read for context) F. DELIVERABLE STRUCTURE (recite VERBATIM: branch name, every test file with its test class/function names and naming conventions, ledger entry) G. ORACLE EXPECTATION (recite VERBATIM the exact git diff --name-only output expected) H. EA-5 ENUMERATION GATE NOTE (confirm you understand this milestone is NOT EA-5 and the enumeration gate does not apply here) I. RISKS AND AMBIGUITIES (list any, including the dev_mode no-op observation) J. PRODUCTION FILE PROHIBITION ACKNOWLEDGMENT (quote the prohibition verbatim)
STOP after posting the comprehension gate. Do NOT proceed to implementation. Wait for SDO VERDICT: APPROVED before starting any work.

milestone.objective: Close all test coverage, assertion-quality, and boundary-verification gaps in the policy_agent service cluster identified by the Sprint 7 audit in docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md. This EA addresses HIGH- and MEDIUM-priority gaps spanning six modules: entrypoint.py (fail-closed error codes + validate_runtime_config + stop isolation), hybrid_adjudicator.py (escalation floor boundary at 0.50), boot.py (exception path + property tests + retry coverage), car.py (sensitivity normalization + parameters_schema propagation), rate_and_resource_rules.py (time-based sliding window expiry), and a new constants test file. No production code is modified. All work is pure test-authoring.
milestone.depends_on: main (c6f429d) — no preceding EA for Sprint 8

item.title: Add last_failure["code"] assertion to test_start_fails_closed_on_rule_config_failure
item.detail:
File: services/policy_agent/tests/test_entrypoint.py Test: TestPolicyAgentEntrypoint::test_start_fails_closed_on_rule_config_failure
Current state: asserts only `start() is False` and `running is False`. Required addition: assert `service.last_failure["code"] == "PA_RULE_CONFIG_LOAD_FAILED"`.
IMPORTANT: Read entrypoint.py carefully before writing the assertion. The test currently patches only `load_rule_engine_config` to return None but does NOT patch `PolicyGPUInference`. Trace the actual boot sequence in entrypoint.py to determine which step fails first and what error code is actually emitted. If the test is currently failing at model load (PA_MODEL_LOAD_FAILED) rather than rule config (PA_RULE_CONFIG_LOAD_FAILED) — because PolicyGPUInference is not mocked and the model dir doesn't exist — the test MUST also be fixed to properly mock PolicyGPUInference so that model load succeeds and the failure occurs at the rule-config step. The test's name and intent must match what it actually tests.
Pattern to follow: test_start_non_dev_fails_closed_when_jwt_key_missing which already asserts `service.last_failure.get("code") == "PA_CFG_JWT_KEY_PATH_NOT_FOUND"`.

item.title: Add last_failure["code"] assertion to test_start_fails_closed_on_model_load_failure
item.detail:
File: services/policy_agent/tests/test_entrypoint.py Test: TestPolicyAgentEntrypoint::test_start_fails_closed_on_model_load_failure
Current state: asserts only `start() is False` and `running is False`. Required addition: assert `service.last_failure["code"] == "PA_MODEL_LOAD_FAILED"`.
Read entrypoint.py `_phase_model_load` to verify the exact error code. The production code at the time of authoring (c6f429d) sets `boot_context["error_code"] = "PA_MODEL_LOAD_FAILED"` when `inference.load_model()` returns False.
Same pattern as WI-1: last_failure["code"] must be explicitly asserted.

item.source: Coverage Map / Exact Escalation Floor Boundary Untested
item.title: Add escalation floor boundary test at confidence == 0.50 to test_hybrid_adjudicator.py
item.detail:
File: services/policy_agent/tests/test_hybrid_adjudicator.py Class: TestPipelineWithMockedNPU (or TestPipelineWithGPUStub — read existing class name)
Add test: test_confidence_at_escalation_floor_escalates confidence = 0.50 (exact lower bound of ESCALATION_CONFIDENCE_RANGE) Expected: AdjudicationDecision.ESCALATE Rationale: ESCALATION_CONFIDENCE_RANGE = (0.50, 0.75); the `>=` lower bound at 0.50 is currently unverified. A typo changing `>=` to `>` would deny at exactly 0.50 without any test catching it.
Read the existing test test_confidence_just_below_threshold_escalates or similar to understand the _make_mocked_adjudicator pattern and mirror it exactly.
ALSO add (under mature_not_minimal authorization, 1-hour cap): test_confidence_just_above_escalation_floor_escalates (confidence=0.51, expected: ESCALATE) — completes the boundary coverage between 0.49 (covered as DENY) and 0.60 (covered as ESCALATE).
Do NOT rename the existing class (e.g. TestPipelineWithNPUStub). Stale NPU naming in class names is EA-5 scope.

item.title: Add exception-in-action path test to test_boot.py
item.detail:
File: services/policy_agent/tests/test_boot.py
Add test: test_run_measured_boot_action_raises_exception_fails_closed Create a MeasuredBootStep whose action() raises RuntimeError("test boot exception"). Call run_measured_boot with this step. Assert: state.ready is False, state.hard_locked is True (after max_attempts exhausted), state.error_code == step.error_code (the step's error_code, NOT "PA_BOOT_UNKNOWN_FAILURE"), state.error_message contains the exception text ("test boot exception").
Read boot.py lines 129-146 (the exception catch block) to confirm exact behavior before writing the assertion. The exception path sets error_code and error_message from the except clause and sets all_steps_passed = False.

item.title: Add BootState.failed_step property tests to test_boot.py
item.detail:
File: services/policy_agent/tests/test_boot.py
Add tests targeting BootState.failed_step property directly (not through run_measured_boot):
test_failed_step_returns_none_when_all_passed: Create BootState with config_loaded=True, attestation_verified=True, weights_verified=True, npu_model_loaded=True, rules_loaded=True, listener_started=True. Assert: state.failed_step is None.
test_failed_step_returns_first_incomplete_field: Create BootState with config_loaded=True, attestation_verified=True, weights_verified=False (all others default False). Assert: state.failed_step == "weights_verified".
test_failed_step_returns_config_loaded_when_nothing_set: Create BootState() with all defaults (all False). Assert: state.failed_step == "config_loaded".
NOTE: Read boot.py BootState.failed_step property to verify the check order (config_loaded, attestation_verified, weights_verified, npu_model_loaded, rules_loaded, listener_started). The field name is "npu_model_loaded" (retained for schema compatibility per boot.py comment — stale naming cleanup is EA-5 scope).

item.title: Add dev_mode parameter effect test to test_boot.py
item.detail:
File: services/policy_agent/tests/test_boot.py
Add test: test_dev_mode_parameter_accepted_without_error Call run_measured_boot with a successful step and dev_mode=True. Call run_measured_boot with the same step and dev_mode=False. Assert both return state.ready is True.
IMPORTANT: Read boot.py run_measured_boot. At HEAD c6f429d, the dev_mode parameter is currently a no-op: `_ = dev_mode`. The test documents the CURRENT behavior. In the completion report, note: "dev_mode parameter is currently not implemented (boot.py line N: `_ = dev_mode`). Test documents no behavioral difference between dev_mode=True and dev_mode=False at this HEAD. Production behavior may diverge in future when attestation bypass is wired."
Do NOT add a "dev_mode=True skips attestation" assertion that doesn't match the production code. Test what the code actually does.

item.title: Add retry_delay_s non-default isolation test to test_boot.py
item.detail:
File: services/policy_agent/tests/test_boot.py
Add test: test_measured_boot_policy_sleep_fn_receives_retry_delay Use a MeasuredBootPolicy(max_attempts=3, retry_delay_s=0.123). Pass a recording lambda as sleep_fn (e.g. captured_sleeps = []; sleep_fn = captured_sleeps.append). Use a step that fails on first attempt, succeeds on second. Assert: len(captured_sleeps) == 1 (one retry between attempt 1 and attempt 2). Assert: captured_sleeps[0] == 0.123 (exact value from policy).
This test pins the sleep injection contract: boot retries use the policy's retry_delay_s value, not a hardcoded constant.

item.source: Assertion Quality / boot.py success test gap
item.title: Add step-field boolean assertions to test_run_measured_boot_success_sets_ready
item.detail:
File: services/policy_agent/tests/test_boot.py
Existing test: test_run_measured_boot_success_sets_ready Current assertions: state.ready is True, state.hard_locked is False, state.attempt_count == 1, and call order verification. Missing assertions: individual step-completion boolean fields.
Add to the existing test (after the current assertions): assert state.config_loaded is True assert state.attestation_verified is True assert state.weights_verified is True assert state.npu_model_loaded is True (field name in boot.py; stale naming is EA-5 scope) assert state.rules_loaded is True assert state.listener_started is True
Rationale: a step that sets the wrong state_field (e.g. a typo in state_field="npu_model_loaded" vs "model_loaded") would pass the current test because only state.ready is checked. These field-level assertions pin the step-to-state mapping.

item.title: Add validate_runtime_config() direct isolation tests to test_entrypoint.py
item.detail:
File: services/policy_agent/tests/test_entrypoint.py
Add new test class: TestValidateRuntimeConfig
test_validate_runtime_config_returns_true_for_valid_dev_config: Write a minimal valid TOML to tmp_path (use the _write_minimal_config helper). Call PolicyAgentService.validate_runtime_config( deployment_mode="host", dev_mode_override=True, config_path=str(config_path) ). Assert: result == (True, None) (no failure fingerprint).
test_validate_runtime_config_returns_false_for_missing_config: Call validate_runtime_config with a config_path pointing to a nonexistent file. Assert: ok is False, fingerprint is not None, fingerprint["code"] starts with "PA_".
Read entrypoint.py validate_runtime_config (classmethod) to understand its call signature and failure modes before writing assertions. The method returns Tuple[bool, dict | None].
NOTE: If validate_runtime_config calls resolve_service_root(__file__, ...) internally and this causes path resolution issues in test context, you may need to patch resolve_service_root or use explicit config_path override. Read the production code first.

item.source: Coverage Map / entrypoint.py stop() not isolated
item.title: Add stop() isolation test to test_entrypoint.py
item.detail:
File: services/policy_agent/tests/test_entrypoint.py
Add test: test_stop_when_not_running_does_not_raise Create a PolicyAgentService with _write_minimal_config. Do NOT call start(). Call service.stop(). Assert: no exception is raised, service.running is False.
Read entrypoint.py stop() method to understand what it does when _running is False. The test documents the no-op behavior when stop() is called on a non-started service.

item.title: Add string sensitivity normalization test to test_car.py
item.detail:
File: services/policy_agent/tests/test_car.py Class: TestCARConstruction
Add test: test_build_car_string_sensitivity Call build_car(..., sensitivity="INTERNAL"). Assert: car.sensitivity == Sensitivity.INTERNAL.
Call build_car(..., sensitivity="PUBLIC"). Assert: car.sensitivity == Sensitivity.PUBLIC.
Call build_car(..., sensitivity="CONFIDENTIAL") if the enum has that value. (Read shared/schemas/car.py Sensitivity enum to verify valid string values.)
Pattern: mirror test_build_car_string_verb which already verifies string verb normalization.
Rationale: string verb is covered, string sensitivity is not. A regression in Sensitivity string normalization would not be caught by any existing test.

item.title: Add parameters_schema field propagation test to test_car.py
item.detail:
File: services/policy_agent/tests/test_car.py Class: TestCARConstruction (or new class TestCAROptionalFields)
FIRST: Read services/policy_agent/src/car.py build_car() signature to verify parameters_schema is an accepted parameter. Read shared/schemas/car.py CanonicalActionRepresentation to verify the field exists.
If the field exists and is propagated by build_car(): Add test: test_build_car_parameters_schema_propagated Call build_car(..., parameters_schema={"arg1": "str", "arg2": "int"}). Assert: car.parameters_schema == {"arg1": "str", "arg2": "int"}.
Add test: test_build_car_parameters_schema_defaults_to_none_or_empty Call build_car() without parameters_schema. Assert the field is None or {} (whichever matches the default).
If parameters_schema is NOT in build_car()'s signature: Skip this WI and note in completion report: "WI-12 N/A — parameters_schema is not a build_car() parameter at HEAD c6f429d; field exists on CAR schema but is not exposed by the builder. No test action required."

item.source: Coverage Map / RateLimiter sliding window expiry
item.title: Add time-based sliding-window expiry test to test_rate_and_resource_rules.py
item.detail:
File: services/policy_agent/tests/test_rate_and_resource_rules.py Class: TestRateLimiter
Add test: test_rate_limiter_sliding_window_evicts_expired_requests Strategy: Use a short window (e.g., window_seconds=0.05) and real time.sleep(). Step 1: Create RateLimiter(max_requests_per_window=2, window_seconds=0.05). Step 2: Record 2 requests for agent "orch" — limiter should be at capacity. Step 3: Verify the third request returns RuleVerdict.DENY (over budget). Step 4: Sleep 0.06 seconds (past the window). Step 5: Record another request — limiter should now accept it (window has expired). Assert: fourth request returns ALLOW (not DENY), confirming old entries were evicted.
Alternative (if time-based test is flaky in CI): use a mock clock. Read services/policy_agent/src/rule_engine.py RateLimiter to understand how timestamps are collected. If it uses time.time(), you can monkeypatch it: monkeypatch.setattr("services.policy_agent.src.rule_engine.time.time", lambda: fake_time) Use a fake_time variable that you advance in the test.
Prefer the monkeypatch approach if available — it avoids real sleep latency. Use real sleep ONLY if the RateLimiter internals are not patchable.
Note: test_rate_and_resource_rules.py imports `time` but never uses it — this import was likely added in anticipation of this test. The test should use the existing module import rather than adding a duplicate.

item.title: Create test_constants_pa.py with direct assertion tests for all PA behavioral constants
item.detail:
File to CREATE: services/policy_agent/tests/test_constants_pa.py
Class: TestPolicyAgentConstants One test per constant value (or a single parametrized test):
test_probabilistic_confidence_threshold: from services.policy_agent.src.constants import PROBABILISTIC_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD assert PROBABILISTIC_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD == 0.75
test_escalation_confidence_range: from services.policy_agent.src.constants import ESCALATION_CONFIDENCE_RANGE assert ESCALATION_CONFIDENCE_RANGE == (0.50, 0.75) assert ESCALATION_CONFIDENCE_RANGE[0] == 0.50 (escalation floor) assert ESCALATION_CONFIDENCE_RANGE[1] == 0.75 (escalation ceiling == confidence threshold)
test_measured_boot_constants: from services.policy_agent.src.constants import MEASURED_BOOT_MAX_ATTEMPTS, MEASURED_BOOT_RETRY_DELAY_S, MEASURED_BOOT_REQUIRED assert MEASURED_BOOT_MAX_ATTEMPTS == 3 assert MEASURED_BOOT_RETRY_DELAY_S == 0.25 assert MEASURED_BOOT_REQUIRED is True
test_jwt_constants: from services.policy_agent.src.constants import JWT_VALIDITY_SECONDS, JWT_ISSUER assert JWT_VALIDITY_SECONDS == 5 assert JWT_ISSUER == "policy_agent"
test_rate_limit_constants: from services.policy_agent.src.constants import RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS, RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS assert RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS == 100 assert RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS == 60.0
test_service_identity_constants: from services.policy_agent.src.constants import SERVICE_NAME, RULE_ENGINE_VERSION assert SERVICE_NAME == "policy_agent" assert RULE_ENGINE_VERSION == "1.0.0"
test_inference_device_constant: from services.policy_agent.src.constants import INFERENCE_DEVICE assert INFERENCE_DEVICE == "GPU" (post-ADR-011; re-exported from shared.constants.PA_DEVICE)
Rationale: A regression changing PROBABILISTIC_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD from 0.75 to 0.80 or ESCALATION_CONFIDENCE_RANGE from (0.50, 0.75) would not be caught by any existing test. The escalation floor (0.50) and confidence threshold (0.75) are the most critical operational decision boundaries in the Policy Agent.

file.test_functions: test_probabilistic_confidence_threshold, test_escalation_confidence_range, test_measured_boot_constants, test_jwt_constants, test_rate_limit_constants, test_service_identity_constants, test_inference_device_constant

file.changes: WI-1: test_start_fails_closed_on_rule_config_failure — add last_failure["code"] assertion (and if necessary, add PolicyGPUInference mock to ensure test tests the right failure path) WI-2: test_start_fails_closed_on_model_load_failure — add last_failure["code"] assertion WI-9: new class TestValidateRuntimeConfig with tests: test_validate_runtime_config_returns_true_for_valid_dev_config test_validate_runtime_config_returns_false_for_missing_config WI-10: test_stop_when_not_running_does_not_raise (in TestPolicyAgentEntrypoint or new class)

WI-4: test_run_measured_boot_action_raises_exception_fails_closed WI-5: test_failed_step_returns_none_when_all_passed test_failed_step_returns_first_incomplete_field test_failed_step_returns_config_loaded_when_nothing_set WI-6: test_dev_mode_parameter_accepted_without_error WI-7: test_measured_boot_policy_sleep_fn_receives_retry_delay WI-8: extend test_run_measured_boot_success_sets_ready with step-field assertions

WI-11: test_build_car_string_sensitivity (added to TestCARConstruction) WI-12: test_build_car_parameters_schema_propagated (if applicable after reading car.py) test_build_car_parameters_schema_defaults_to_none_or_empty (if applicable)

Add Entry 51: Task 8 / EA-1: Policy Agent Test Hardening — COMPLETE Format: follow the existing entry format exactly. Include: branch name, commit hash, net new test count, baseline before/after, date.

deliverable.naming_conventions: Test class names: PascalCase, descriptive (TestPolicyAgentConstants, TestValidateRuntimeConfig). Test function names: snake_case, starts with test_, describes the behavior being tested. No numbered prefixes on section headers (L-12 compliance). No extra sections beyond what is listed in this deliverable (L-12 compliance).

negative_constraints.constraint: DO NOT modify any file outside tests/, conftest.py, docs/, or pyproject.toml. If a production code change is required to unblock a test, STOP, document the blocker in your completion report, and wait for SDO direction. Do not make the change unilaterally.

DO NOT rename stale NPU identifiers (class names TestPipelineWithNPUStub, TestEndToEndWithNPUStub, helper _make_npu_stub, etc.). This is EA-5 scope. Your tests may reference these class names (to add tests to them) but must not rename them.
DO NOT author EA-2, EA-3, EA-4, or EA-5 work. Only policy_agent tests are in scope for this EA.
DO NOT add ISS-3 (PA stop-token mismatch) tests. ISS-3 requires production code changes and is explicitly deferred per SDV §13.1.

DO NOT add sections, headers, or content that is not part of the deliverable contract in Section 5 (L-12 compliance). Do not rename required sections. Do not add numbered prefixes to sections in your comprehension gate response.
DO NOT use parallel EA execution. This is the only active EA. No other EA may be authored or executed concurrently.
DO NOT add integration tests requiring a live Hyper-V VM or real GPU inference. All new tests must be unit-scoped and runnable under the REGRESSION pytest scope.

DO NOT add a new conftest.py unless absolutely necessary for fixtures that serve multiple test files. If needed, create at services/policy_agent/tests/conftest.py only. Do NOT create a root-level conftest.py.

quality_gate.gate: After authoring all tests, verify no import errors: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI .venv\Scripts\python -c "import services.policy_agent.tests.test_constants_pa" No ImportError or ModuleNotFoundError is acceptable.

Run the REGRESSION test suite: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI .venv\Scripts\pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q PASS criteria: test count MUST NOT decrease below 755 passed, 2 skipped. Any new test must appear in the count (count should increase by ≥ 10). No pre-existing tests may regress.

Verify no production files were modified: git diff main...feature/p5-task8-ea1-policy-agent-hardening --name-only | grep -vE "tests|conftest|docs|pyproject" Expected output: EMPTY (zero lines). If ANY production file appears, the EA has violated Sprint 8's mandate. STOP and report the violation in the completion report before doing anything else.

verification.description: These are verbatim terminal commands the Lead Architect can run to confirm success (non-developer verification). Run from C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI.

step.expected: Most recent commit should be the EA-1 task8 commit (test count in message)
Only test files, docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md — no production source files
≥ 755 passed, 2 skipped. The exact count increase appears in the EA commit message.

step.command: git diff main...feature/p5-task8-ea1-policy-agent-hardening --name-only | findstr /v "tests conftest docs pyproject"
step.expected: Empty output (no production files in the diff)
All policy_agent tests pass, including all new WI-1 through WI-14 tests

commit_template.format: test(task8/ea1): policy_agent boundary + isolation + constants hardening — {N} new tests, baseline 755 → {Y}
commit_template.example: test(task8/ea1): policy_agent boundary + isolation + constants hardening — 18 new tests, baseline 755 → 773
commit_template.rules: - N = exact count of new tests added by this EA (verify with pytest --co -q diff) - Y = total passed count after this EA's changes (run full regression suite) - Commit only files under services/policy_agent/tests/ and docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md - Do NOT commit any production source files

mature_not_minimal.directive:
EA-1 is authorized to address directly adjacent findings discovered during work — even if not pre-listed in docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md — provided each new item adds ≤ 1 hour of additional work.
Examples of in-scope adjacent work: - While adding last_failure["code"] assertions (WI-1, WI-2), you discover a third fail-closed test with the same gap. Fix it. - While reading boot.py for WI-4, you notice an edge case in the policy validation that has no test. Add it if the test is straightforward and ≤ 1 hour. - While adding the constants test (WI-14), you realize a shared constant used by the PA (e.g., from shared.constants) should also be value-anchored. Add it.
Items exceeding the 1-hour cap per new item: - Document in your completion report as a new finding. - SDO will open a Vikunja task for the next sprint. - Do NOT absorb them silently into this EA.
This is NOT a license for unbounded scope. EA-1 addresses policy_agent ONLY. AO, semantic_router, UI, shared, and launcher are EA-2 through EA-4 scope.

file.sections: policy_agent coverage gaps, policy_agent assertion quality, policy_agent boundary violations, HIGH priority gap items 1-3, MEDIUM priority policy_agent items
file.reason: Complete input specification for EA-1 scope — read the policy_agent sections in full
Required for WI-1, WI-2, WI-9, WI-10 — read start(), stop(), validate_runtime_config() in full
Required for WI-4 through WI-8 — read run_measured_boot(), BootState, MeasuredBootStep, failed_step property
Required for WI-14 — read all constant values
Required for WI-11, WI-12 — verify build_car() signature for sensitivity and parameters_schema
Required for WI-11, WI-12 — verify CanonicalActionRepresentation fields and Sensitivity enum
Required for WI-13 — read RateLimiter internals to plan the mock clock approach
Required for WI-1, WI-2, WI-9, WI-10 — understand existing test structure
Required for WI-4 through WI-8 — understand existing test structure
Required for WI-3 — understand existing adjudicator test structure and _make_mocked_adjudicator pattern
Required for WI-13 — understand TestRateLimiter structure
Required for WI-11, WI-12 — understand TestCARConstruction structure
Required for Entry 51 — follow the existing entry format exactly

file.sections: §4 Success Criteria, §5.1 EA-1 scope, §5.2 Out-of-scope, §5.3 Edge cases
file.reason: Strategic alignment baseline — EA-1 scope must align with SDV §5.1
